prompt: Your defender turns his head on the wing. What is your first read?

choices.label: Hard backdoor cut to the rim
choices.feedback_text: Correct. Head turn gives you a direct window to the rim.

choices.label: Jog to the top for a reset
choices.feedback_text: You pass up the advantage and reset a winning moment.

choices.label: Stand and call for the ball
choices.feedback_text: Calling for it lets the defender recover vision and position.

choices.label: Screen away from the action
choices.feedback_text: Screening away ignores the immediate scoring lane.

explanation_md: You read the advantage early and take the highest-value action first. In this concept, timing and spacing decide whether the offense or defense keeps control. The right answer protects the primary priority, then flows into the next rotation or pass window. This rep targets cutting relocation at level 1.

prompt: You set a pass and screen away. After your teammate catches, what next?

choices.label: Hold screen contact and watch
choices.feedback_text: Holding contact too long becomes stationary and clogs movement.

choices.label: Relocate to open space for return pass
choices.feedback_text: Correct. Relocation after screen creates the second action window.

choices.label: Cut to same side corner
choices.feedback_text: Same-side drift can stack two players and kill the lane.

choices.label: Trail behind the ball slowly
choices.feedback_text: Trailing late removes timing from the give-and-go sequence.

explanation_md: You read the advantage early and take the highest-value action first. In this concept, timing and spacing decide whether the offense or defense keeps control. The right answer protects the primary priority, then flows into the next rotation or pass window. This rep targets cutting relocation at level 1.

prompt: Ball-handler drives baseline from your side. You are strong-side corner. Best move?

choices.label: Stay corner and be ready
choices.feedback_text: Correct. Staying deep gives the driver a clear kickout lane.

choices.label: Lift to wing as drive starts
choices.feedback_text: Lifting opens the baseline help to sink without consequence.

choices.label: Cut through paint toward opposite dunker
choices.feedback_text: That cut crowds the finish and brings your defender to the rim.

choices.label: Set a brush screen for the driver
choices.feedback_text: A brush screen risks an offensive foul in a tight lane.

explanation_md: You read the advantage early and take the highest-value action first. In this concept, timing and spacing decide whether the offense or defense keeps control. The right answer protects the primary priority, then flows into the next rotation or pass window. This rep targets cutting relocation at level 1.

prompt: After you pass from slot to wing, defender denies your return lane. Counter?

choices.label: V-cut then pop back to slot
choices.feedback_text: Correct. V-cut changes angle and timing for clean relocation.

choices.label: Stand and wait for reversal
choices.feedback_text: Waiting lets denial pressure kill the flow.

choices.label: Cut straight to low block
choices.feedback_text: Low-block cut is low value from this perimeter action.

choices.label: Chase the ball for a handoff
choices.feedback_text: Chasing ball compresses space and invites a trap.

explanation_md: You read the advantage early and take the highest-value action first. In this concept, timing and spacing decide whether the offense or defense keeps control. The right answer protects the primary priority, then flows into the next rotation or pass window. This rep targets cutting relocation at level 2.

prompt: Your 45-cut lane is open as the ball enters the post. Best timing?

choices.label: Cut immediately on the pass flight
choices.feedback_text: Correct. Cutting on flight beats weak-side help rotations.

choices.label: Delay until post catches and faces
choices.feedback_text: Delayed cut gives help time to tag and recover.

choices.label: Wait for a dribble then cut
choices.feedback_text: Waiting for dribble often closes the window.

choices.label: Never cut on post touches
choices.feedback_text: You lose a core relocation rule if you never cut here.

explanation_md: You read the advantage early and take the highest-value action first. In this concept, timing and spacing decide whether the offense or defense keeps control. The right answer protects the primary priority, then flows into the next rotation or pass window. This rep targets cutting relocation at level 2.
